report_name: State-Sponsored APT 'ChronoDragon' Targets Global Financials with 'CoinThief' Backdoor

threat_actors.name: ChronoDragon
threat_actors.description: A state-sponsored Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) group conducting an economic espionage campaign against the global financial sector. The group uses the custom CoinThief backdoor to steal sensitive non-public information, such as M&A details and proprietary trading algorithms.
threat_actors.goals: To gain strategic economic insights by stealing confidential business information, such as details on mergers and acquisitions (M&A), proprietary trading algorithms, and confidential market strategies.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: Used for initial access by sending carefully crafted emails with malicious Microsoft Office attachments to high-value employees within financial institutions.

attack_patterns.name: System Binary Proxy Execution
attack_patterns.description: The CoinThief backdoor leverages legitimate system tools and processes to perform tasks, blending in with normal network activity to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The CoinThief backdoor was used to steal documents and other sensitive files over its command and control channel.

attack_patterns.name: Screen Capture
attack_patterns.description: The CoinThief backdoor has the capability to monitor user activity by capturing the screen.

attack_patterns.name: Keylogging
attack_patterns.description: The CoinThief backdoor is capable of capturing credentials and other typed information via keystroke logging.

malware.name: CoinThief
malware.description: A new, custom, multi-stage backdoor used by ChronoDragon for stealthy infiltration of financial institutions. It uses multiple layers of obfuscation and has capabilities for data exfiltration, screen capture, and keylogging.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used for obfuscated or hidden command execution as part of Living-off-the-Land techniques employed by the attackers.

vulnerabilities.name: Microsoft Office Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A recently patched vulnerability in Microsoft Office was exploited via malicious attachments to drop and execute the initial stage of the CoinThief malware.

identities.name: Financial Institutions
identities.description: A class of organizations targeted by the ChronoDragon APT, including banks, investment firms, and cryptocurrency exchanges.

indicators.name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Associated with ChronoDragon
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Associated with ChronoDragon
id: 9b1b3c4d-1a2b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of obfuscated or hidden PowerShell windows, a technique associated with ChronoDragon's CoinThief backdoor.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/chronodragon-apt-targets-financials
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-26
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -w hidden'
      - ' -nop '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of obfuscated or hidden PowerShell windows, a technique associated with ChronoDragon's CoinThief backdoor, based on hunting hints provided in the report.

indicators.name: Microsoft Office Application Spawning Child Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Microsoft Office Application Spawning Child Process
id: 8a2b4c5d-2b3c-4d6e-9f0a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects Microsoft Office applications spawning command-line interpreters, a common TTP for initial access via malicious documents as used by ChronoDragon.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/chronodragon-apt-targets-financials
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-26
tags:
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects Microsoft Office applications spawning command-line interpreters, a common TTP for initial access via malicious documents as used by ChronoDragon.

indicators.name: Executable File Creation in Public Directories
indicators.pattern:
title: Executable File Creation in Public Directories
id: 7b3c5d6e-3c4d-4e7f-a0b1-2c3d4e5f6a7b
status: experimental
description: Detects malware payloads being dropped in world-writable directories, a technique potentially used by the CoinThief backdoor.
references:
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/chronodragon-apt-targets-financials
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-26
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|startswith:
      - 'C:\Users\Public\'
      - 'C:\ProgramData\'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects malware payloads being dropped in world-writable directories, a technique potentially used by the CoinThief backdoor.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious PowerShell Execution Associated with ChronoDragon
Microsoft Office Application Spawning Child Process
Executable File Creation in Public Directories
